The thing about these third-party or compatible IBM PC BIOSes is to make them compatible with the original IBM BIOS, all the entry points to the various interrupt routines which are sort of the handlers for various functions of the BIOS need to be at the exact same location in the ROM space as the original ROM. Otherwise, any program that will call these things directly by a memory location thinking that they're on an actual IBM PC are going to fail to run.
